Instead of feeding you mindless content, Soch delivers an endless stream of AI-curated research articles — each one paired with a visually engaging image, a title designed to spark curiosity, and a short summary to draw you in. Whether you’re looking to explore new ideas, stay mentally stimulated, or just replace your doomscrolling habit with something more enriching, Soch offers a fresh, modern way to engage with content that matters. From evaluation to production in 12 weeks .
website.text_excerpt: F/AI Your agents act. Rippletide keeps them accountable. Product Automatic Ontologies Context Graph Agent Evaluation Pricing Developers Start building Docs Enterprise Decision Infrastructure Use Cases AI Agent Governance Company About Blog Careers F/AI Press Contact Start building Book a demo 🏆 We won the OpenAI Codex Hackathon in San Francisco! Read the story → 🚀 Rippletide pitched at F/AI Deal Day @ Station F Read the takeaways → Automatic ontologies = enforceable rules, zero schema work Your AI agents are starting to act. Who is accountable when they do? Right now, agents approve refunds, trigger workflows, and make compliance calls, often without deterministic control. Rippletide is the runtime reinforcement infrastructure that enforces every decision before it runs in production. Your rules. Actually enforced. Wrong? Blocked. Right? Executed. From autonomous agents to accountable agents. RUNTIME REINFORCEMENT, ENTERPRISE INFRASTRUCTURE FOR ACCOUNTABLE AI Book a Demo Talk to our engineers +15 pts agent outcome 2 weeks to bootstrap 350ms evaluation Agents start by answering questions. Then they resolve tickets. Eventually they need to take real actions. Refund a customer Modify an account Cancel a shift Approve a payment Merge a pull request That's where most companies stop. Not because the model isn't capable. Because nobody owns the decision. Rippletide becomes the decision runtime before execution. TRUSTED FOR AGENTS BY The Missing Layer . Every AI agent makes decisions. Almost none can prove they're right. The problem isn't intelligence. It's trust. Your LLM is brilliant. It can reason, plan, and execute across your entire stack. But brilliant isn't enough when it's approving refunds, triggering workflows, or making compliance-sensitive calls. The 85% that works...
